title: Research Run
description: A research task execution. Status mirrors the MCP task lifecycle (working, completed, failed, cancelled). Progress and report are updated by the worker in real time.

title.description: Short human label for the run (3–8 words). Distinct from `query`, which holds the full research brief. Auto-generated server-side via the FAST_LLM shortly after the entity is created when not supplied by the caller. Renders in list rows and as the detail-view heading; the UI falls back to a truncated `query` while title is null.

run_status.description: Execution status, mirrors the MCP task status for the underlying run. Distinct from the base entity `status` field which tracks lifecycle (active/archived/deleted).

status_message.description: Short human-readable description of the current phase

report.description: The final markdown report. Populated when status becomes 'completed'.

sources.description: Sources consulted during the research run. Populated incrementally during the 'Gathering sources' phase.

snippet.description: Short excerpt or summary of the source (optional)

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the run started

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the run reached a terminal state

last_heartbeat_at.description: Last time the worker emitted any signal, including liveness heartbeats. Updated continuously while the run is working — independent of progress, which only advances on real phase transitions. Used by the UI to surface staleness when no signal has arrived in 10s+ (separates 'still alive but slow' from 'actually hung').

current_phase_started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp recording when the currently-active phase began. The UI derives the elapsed-time suffix in the status line from this — `now - current_phase_started_at`, recomputed every second client-side. This decouples display cadence from worker write cadence: the worker only updates this value on phase transitions (a stable input), the UI animates the display (the smooth output). Cleared on terminal transitions.

phase_history.description: Timeline of phase transitions for this run. Append-only; each entry records when a phase started and when it ended (null for the currently-running phase). Preserved after completion so the time distribution is auditable forever.

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this phase began

ended_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this phase ended; null if currently running
